varieties.name: Pterocarya stenoptera (Chinese Wingnut)
varieties.origin: Eastern and central China, widespread distribution from Shaanxi to Zhejiang provinces. Introduced to Western cultivation 1862.
varieties.growing_notes: Generally robust with no serious pests or diseases. Tolerates waterlogging and flooding. Some susceptibility to late spring frosts (not relevant for Brisbane).

varieties.name: Pterocarya fraxinifolia (Caucasian Wingnut)
varieties.origin: Caucasus region (Georgia, Azerbaijan), Turkey, northern Iran. Grows in relict Tertiary forests. Introduced to France 1782, Britain circa 1800.
varieties.growing_notes: Generally healthy but susceptible to late frost damage (not an issue in Brisbane). Can hybridize with P. stenoptera if both present.

varieties.name: Pterocarya × rehderiana (Rehder's Wingnut)
varieties.origin: Hybrid first occurred spontaneously in cultivation at Arnold Arboretum, Boston (1879 seed from France). Named 1906 for botanist Alfred Rehder.
varieties.growing_notes: Robust hybrid with enhanced disease resistance compared to parents. No serious pest issues.

varieties.name: Pterocarya hupehensis (Hubei Wingnut)
varieties.origin: Hubei, Henan, and Sichuan provinces, China. Grows in mountainous areas. Collected by Ernest Wilson 1901, Augustine Henry 1888.

varieties.name: Pterocarya rhoifolia (Japanese Wingnut)
varieties.origin: Japan (Honshu, Hokkaido), South Korea, Eastern China (Shandong). Riparian forests. Introduced to cultivation by Duke of Bedford 1889.

varieties.name: Pterocarya macroptera (Big-winged Wingnut)
varieties.origin: Central and southern China (scattered populations). Grows at 1100-3500m elevation in upper river valleys. Three varieties recognized.

varieties.name: Pterocarya tonkinensis (Tonkin Wingnut)
varieties.origin: Vietnam (north and central), Laos, southern Yunnan (China). Indigenous to tropical monsoon forests and floodplains, 200-1200m elevation (typically below 700m). Most southerly distribution of any wingnut - extends to 17°N latitude in Laos.

varieties.name: Pterocarya stenoptera (Chinese Wingnut)
varieties.origin: Eastern and central China - very widespread distribution from Shaanxi to Zhejiang.

varieties.name: P. × rehderiana (Hybrid): Hybrid of two temperate species. ❌ Not viable
